title: podman-2.2.0-rc1: "Error: could not get either XDG_CONFIG_HOME or HOME"
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I've just updated from a (heavily patched) `podman-2.1.1` to `podman-2.2.0-rc1`, and now on attempting to run a container I've getting the message:

```
Error: could not get either XDG_CONFIG_HOME or HOME
```

In this case, the container is being invoked from from an OpenRC init script, so may be subject to a restricted set of environment variables.

Should a variant of HOME not being accessible be an error, though, or simply a warning?  I recall that overrides to the system configuration for certain elements could be placed in per-user dot-files, but should their absence prevent podman from launching the container at all?

(If it makes a difference, this is a system not running `systemd`)

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.0-rc1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Git Commit:   02843f881f9271440e7eaad8db231ddf20e33e51
Built:        Thu Nov 19 22:59:08 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.18.0
  cgroupManager: cgroupfs

  conmon:

    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.21, commit: 35a2fa83022e56e18af7e6a865ba5d7165fa2a4a'
  cpus: 8
  distribution:
    distribution: gentoo
    version: unknown
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: dellr330
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 5.9.6-gentoo
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 1757749248
  memTotal: 33392836608
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: Unknown
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.15.1
      commit: eb0145e5ad4d8207e84a327248af76663d4e50dd
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 25669844992
  swapTotal: 25769787392
  uptime: 222h 16m 40.57s (Approximately 9.25 days)

  search:
  - docker.io
  - docker.pkg.github.com
  - quay.io
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 30
    paused: 0
    running: 25
    stopped: 5
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.ignore_chown_errors: "false"
  graphRoot: /space/podman/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 386
  runRoot: /space/podman/run
  volumePath: /space/podman/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 2.1.0
  Built: 1605826748

  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 2.2.0-rc1

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

comments.body: We believe this is fixed now - hoping to get an RC2 out by end of day with the fix included.

... any luck with the RC2?  This one's a little inconvenient, and if there's another RC soon I'd prefer not to have to make a temporary fix to numerous scripts...

Alternatively, do you know the commit which fixed this issue?  Patching RC1 would also be fine.  I had a quick scan through, but couldn't see the change which should fix this.

Looks like rc1 is still in kubic repositories:
```
$ apt-cache policy podman
podman:
  Installed: 2.2.0~0.1+nmu1
  Candidate: 2.2.0~0.1+nmu1
  Version table:
 *** 2.2.0~0.1+nmu1 500
        500 https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/testing/Debian_10  Packages
        100 /var/lib/dpkg/status
```

Looking forward to the rc2 there.
RC2 should be released this week, but the Holiday might cause issues.

> RC2 should be released this week, but the Holiday might cause issues.

Thanks. In the meanwhile, is there a way to get podman 2.1 deb, equivalent to what's https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/testing/Debian_10/, but for 2.1?

Podman 2.0.6 (what's now available in official debian repos) is a bit too big of a jump for me by now.

There is a non testing version of that repo that tracks the latest stable
release. It's linked in our install page.

> 
> Thanks. In the meanwhile, is there a way to get podman 2.1 deb, equivalent to what's https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/testing/Debian_10/, but for 2.1?
> 
> Podman 2.0.6 (what's now available in official debian repos) is a bit too big of a jump for me by now.

@motiejus you can get the debianized tarball podman_2.1.1~2.tar.gz from https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:testing/podman?rev=243 and run `dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc` in it to generate the 2.1.1 packages.

> @motiejus you can get the debianized tarball podman_2.1.1~2.tar.gz from https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:testing/podman?rev=243 and run dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc in it to generate the 2.1.1 packages.

Thanks a lot, that worked.
Since this is fixed in master, I am closing.  Hope to have fix out this week.
